metadata.title: Rijksmuseum Suriname Collection
metadata.description: Explore the Suriname collection of the Rijksmuseum — paintings, prints, photographs, maps and objects connected to the colonial history of Suriname.

statistics.title: The Data Behind the Collection
statistics.subtitle: A critical look at the Rijksmuseum's Suriname dataset — what we received, what we changed, and what the source itself reveals about whose history was recorded.

sectionSource.title: A colonial dataset, catalogued in Amsterdam
sectionSource.lead: Everything that follows starts from a single CSV file: the Rijksmuseum's export of objects in its collection associated with Suriname. That file is not a neutral mirror of Surinamese history — it is a museum catalogue, written in Dutch, by an Amsterdam institution, describing objects that were made, bought, sold or captured during and after Dutch colonial rule.
sectionSource.body: The statistics on this page treat that source as evidence in two ways. The Raw dataset section shows the catalogue as we received it, before any curation. The Curated dataset section shows what we changed by enriching it with geocoded locations, Wikidata identifiers and Wikimedia Commons images. The deltas in between are themselves a record of where the original metadata fell short.

sectionRaw.title: What the Rijksmuseum gave us
sectionRaw.subtitle: Counts computed directly from the CSV export — no enrichment, no corrections.

sectionDeltas.title: From catalogue to showable record
sectionDeltas.subtitle: How many objects survive each step of the curation pipeline.
sectionDeltas.body: Each bar narrows the dataset by one constraint. The drop from 'has a geographic keyword' to 'has a location specifically inside Suriname' shows how often the original metadata only points to 'Suriname (Zuid-Amerika)' or to a Dutch place. The final bar — 'Showable on the map' — is the strict slice we use on the landing page: a public-domain image, a usable IIIF URL, a year, and a specific point inside Suriname.

sectionCurated.title: What we made of it
sectionCurated.subtitle: The same collection after geocoding, Wikidata linking and license normalisation.

sectionMissing.title: What the data still does not say
sectionMissing.body: Even after curation, the catalogue carries the silences of the institution that produced it:
sectionMissing.bullet1: {count} objects have no named maker — Indigenous, enslaved and African-Surinamese creators were rarely credited.
sectionMissing.bullet2: {count} objects still cannot be placed at a specific point inside Suriname; they are tagged only with the country as a whole or with the harbour cities the objects passed through.
sectionMissing.bullet3: {count} objects either have no image we are allowed to publish, or no image at all — copyright and access decisions are made far from Paramaribo.
sectionMissing.bullet4: Descriptive language in the catalogue reflects 17th–20th century European perspectives; subject terms like 'oorspronkelijke bewoners' or 'overzeese geschiedenis' frame the objects from a colonial vantage point.
